title: Add per-asset orthogonal alt-data features to regime dossiers (break the single-driver hypothesis bottleneck)
why: A controlled persona experiment (base/afinada/v3/v4 x 3 regimes x k=5 draws, DA per draw) showed the binding constraint on hypothesis quality and diversity is NOT the agent prompt but the orthogonal feature set. XAUUSD has only 3 orthogonal features (volume, cot_net_noncomm_z52w, real_yield_5y_z252d), with real_yield the only rich driver, so every persona gravitates to the same real-yield carry story. The double-counting (same driver in primary AND meta) and sparsity (single-condition primary starves the WF event floor) failure modes are the two faces of this single-driver bottleneck: anti-sparsity prompting (v3) pushes toward double-counting; anti-double-counting prompting (v4) pushes toward sparsity and collapses primary diversity. The multi-asset build made it worse, not better: COT/real_yield exist ONLY for the two metals; crypto/fx dossiers carry only `volume` as orthogonal.
value_rationale: This is the actual lever for both hypothesis creativity and escaping the double-counting/sparsity trade-off — more impactful than any further persona tuning, which has hit a feature-set ceiling.
scope: TIER 1 (quick win, NO new data — implement first): inject already-computed orthogonal PIT features from pipeline/features.py into build_dossier_features (phase5/regime_stats.py). Candidates: atr_14_norm, bb_width, rv_term_structure (rv_5/rv_20), rsi_14, macd_hist (different timescales than regime windows), plus cross-asset macro (dxy_z252, vix_level, vix_chg_5, us_2y10y_spread, breakeven_5y) which apply to ALL asset classes incl crypto/fx that currently expose only `volume`.

Vet each candidate with the EXISTING anti-circularity machinery: build_dossier_features already computes correlation_with_regime_features and flags quasi_circular when |spearman rho| vs {roc_63,ma_50,ma_200,rv_20} > QUASI_CIRCULAR_RHO (0.35); circular ones (likely bb_width, rv_term_structure vs rv_20) are auto-excluded from orthogonal_features. Keep only those that pass.

PIT discipline: reuse the same rolling/.shift conventions as build_tier2_features; macro via macro_fetch with .shift(1) publication lag. Add tests (TDD) asserting injected features are PIT and that quasi_circular exclusion fires.

TIER 2 (needs external data sources, separate effort): per-asset alt-data — crypto perp funding / basis; fx rate differentials (FRED) / risk-reversal skew; metals term-structure / lease rates / ETF flows. Each needs a cache + PIT handling + tests.

DEFERRED COMPANION (unchanged): adopt the v4 anti-double-counting hypothesizer guardrail ONLY once a dossier carries >=2 strong orthogonal drivers (Tier 1 likely delivers this for metals; crypto/fx via vix/dxy).
recommendation: Do TIER 1 first via TDD — it is the highest-value, lowest-cost slice and directly breaks the single-driver bottleneck for crypto/fx (vix/dxy) and adds atr/macd levers for metals, reusing the existing quasi_circular vetting so no look-ahead/circular feature can slip in. Confirm the candidate feature list before wiring (it is look-ahead-sensitive). Tier 2 is a follow-on once Tier 1 lands.
out_of_scope: Further persona prompt tuning; it has hit the feature-set ceiling.

history.reason: grounded two-tier plan after reading features.py + regime_stats.py
from: Add asset-class-appropriate orthogonal alt-data to the dossier feature set and the pipeline features: crypto -> perp funding / basis, on-chain flow; fx -> rate differentials, DXY, risk-reversal skew; metals -> term-structure / lease rates, ETF flows beyond GLD.
Ensure each new feature is PIT (.shift for publication lag) and passes the anti-circularity orthogonality check vs roc_63/ma_50/ma_200/rv_20.
Re-run the regime_stats dossier build so orthogonal_features lists the new features.

DEFERRED COMPANION: the v4 hypothesizer guardrail ('a feature either drives the primary OR is a meta feature, never both') cleanly eliminated double-counting at zero block-rate cost in testing, but collapsed creativity because only one rich driver exists. Adopt the v4 guardrail ONLY once a dossier has >=2 strong orthogonal drivers, so the meta has independent information to see. Until then keep the 'afinada' persona.

history.from: Prioritize for any asset we actually want Loop A to explore beyond gold. Production hypothesizer persona stays 'afinada' (richest balance of block-rate, creativity, failure profile). Do NOT adopt v3 (regressed) or v4 (lateral move) yet.
history.to: Do TIER 1 first via TDD — it is the highest-value, lowest-cost slice and directly breaks the single-driver bottleneck for crypto/fx (vix/dxy) and adds atr/macd levers for metals, reusing the existing quasi_circular vetting so no look-ahead/circular feature can slip in.
